title: Malaria-Instruct: A Low-Resource Benchmark for Instruction-Tuned LLMs in Antimalarial Drug Discovery
authors: Ajala, M., Adesina, C.& Ashimiyu-Abdusalam, Z.
excerpt: We present Malaria-Instruct, a specialized dataset designed to evaluate and fine-tune Large Language Models (LLMs) for molecular property prediction, specifically targeting antimalarial activity.
abstract:
While derived from ChEMBL, this dataset has been significantly restructured, filtered, and formatted for instruction-tuning and virtual screening tasks in resource-constrained research environments.

Key Features:

- Instruction-Response Format: Data is structured as natural language prompts (SMILES strings as input, binary activity as output) to align with the training objectives of modern LLMs (e.g., Gemma, LlaSMO, Mistral).
- Rigorous Dissimilarity Splitting: Unlike standard random or scaffol

title: Rigorous Evaluation of Large Language Models for Malaria Drug Discovery: Trade-offs in Performance, Scale, and Resource Utility
authors: Ajala, M., Ashimiyu-Abdusalam, Z. & Adesina, C.
excerpt: We present a systematic evaluation of five open-source LLMs on Malaria-Instruct using a rigorous out-of-distribution data split
abstract:
We present a systematic evaluation of five open-source LLMs (Gemma-2 2B, Gemma-2 9B,
TxGemma-2B, TxGemma-9B, and LlaSMol-Mistral-7B) on Malaria-Instruct, a curated instruction-following dataset derived from the ChEMBL Legacy Malaria corpus for Malaria virtual screening, on a rigorous out-of-distribution data split, benchmarked against classical ML models (Random Forest, XGBoost) and frontier proprietary models (Gemini 2.5, OpenAI o3) under few-shot conditions.
